name: Garden Grove Unified School District
profileTitle: GGUSD District Profile for Calendar Planning
districtFact: Garden Grove Unified School District, also known as GGUSD, serves about 40,000 TK-12 students in Garden Grove, Anaheim, Santa Ana, Westminster, and nearby Orange County communities. GGUSD publishes school-year calendar dates as official online lists rather than PDF downloads for the years covered here, so families should use the district calendar page as the source of record.

about.title: About Garden Grove Unified School District
about.content: Garden Grove Unified School District, also known as GGUSD, serves TK-12 students in Garden Grove, Anaheim, Santa Ana, Westminster, and nearby Orange County communities. The district calendar page lists official school-year dates used by families to plan attendance, transportation, childcare, holidays, recess periods, parent conference dates, staff development days, and student-free days.

about.title: How to Use This GGUSD Calendar
about.content: This page summarizes the official GGUSD online calendar lists in a searchable format. Because no PDF links were provided for these school years, families should use the district calendar page and campus announcements for the official record and any revisions.

planningTips.title: Planning Tips for Garden Grove Unified Families
content: Use the official GGUSD calendar page as the source of record because the supplied calendars are online lists, not PDFs.
Add student-free and staff development days separately because they may create childcare needs outside major recesses.
Check parent conference and minimum-day notices with your school because campus schedules can add details beyond the district list.
Confirm athletic, testing, graduation, and program-specific dates through campus announcements.

compareIntro: Compare the Garden Grove Unified (GGUSD) school calendar with Irvine Unified (IUSD), Long Beach Unified (LBUSD), and Santa Ana Unified (SAUSD) calendars currently available on MySchoolDates. Families often compare nearby districts when planning relocation, enrollment, childcare, shared activities, or cross-district travel. Dates shown include the first day, last day, and spring recess.

districtFaqs.q: Which Garden Grove Unified calendar does this page summarize?
districtFaqs.a: This page summarizes the official GGUSD online school calendar lists for 2025-2026 and 2026-2027.

districtFaqs.q: Does Garden Grove Unified provide a PDF calendar?
districtFaqs.a: No PDF links were supplied for the reviewed GGUSD calendars. This page links to the official district calendar webpage instead.

districtFaqs.q: Where should families verify GGUSD calendar updates?
districtFaqs.a: Use the official GGUSD school calendar page and campus announcements for final confirmation. MySchoolDates is an independent reference and is not affiliated with Garden Grove Unified.

districtFaqs.q: Is the GGUSD calendar the same for elementary and high schools?
districtFaqs.a: Most districtwide dates apply across Garden Grove Unified elementary, middle, intermediate, and high schools, but individual campuses may publish additional events, minimum days, testing schedules, or activity dates. Families should confirm school-specific details with their campus.

districtFaqs.q: Are GGUSD teacher workdays included on this calendar?
districtFaqs.a: Teacher workdays, staff development days, and student-free days are included when they appear in the reviewed official GGUSD calendar list. Campus-specific staff or activity schedules may appear only in school announcements.

customSections.label: Schools Covered by the GGUSD Calendar
customSections.content: This calendar focuses on districtwide dates for Garden Grove Unified School District schools, including elementary, middle, intermediate, and high school campuses unless individual school announcements provide different dates.

groups.label: Use campus notices for
items: Minimum-day schedules that vary by school.
Testing windows, activities, athletics, and graduation details.
Program-specific dates that do not appear on the districtwide calendar.
Same-day updates or schedule changes from the district or school.
